blocks.statement: I can identify my own weak areas from Unit 1 and make a concrete plan to fix them before the transfer task.

blocks.connection: Before the transfer task, every analyst checks their tools. Today you take stock of your Unit 1 toolkit — vectors, F = ma, free-body diagrams, friction, equilibrium — and shore up the weak spots.

blocks.markdown:
## Workshop day — you run it

No new content today. This is your chance to **close gaps** before the transfer task. Work through it honestly and at your own pace.

1. Rate yourself on each Unit 1 target below.
2. Pick the **one or two** you feel weakest on.
3. Re-do practice on those targets (re-open earlier days, or the reader).
4. Write down a concrete plan.

blocks.title: How to use today
blocks.markdown: For each target, ask yourself: *could I do this on a test, with no help, right now?* If not, that target is where your time should go. Re-open the day it came from and re-try the Solve-it and Draw-it tasks — they save again, so your teacher sees the fresh attempt.

blocks.prompt: Pick the target you rated lowest and prove to yourself you have it now: write a short problem on that skill (a vector resultant, an F = ma calculation, or a net-force-from-an-FBD problem) and solve it fully.
blocks.givenHint: List what your self-made problem gives you.
blocks.equationHint: Use the equation for the skill you chose: R = sqrt(x² + y²), F = ma, or F_net = sum of forces.

blocks.frame: My weakest target is ___. My plan to fix it before the transfer task is ___.
blocks.prompt: What target do you still feel weakest on, and what is your concrete plan to fix it before the transfer task?
